rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ENID J. THOMAS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #13, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/24/2023. The following procedural issues to be addressed: 1. Need allegation why the beneficiaries were overpaid. 2. Need clarification of duration and amount of the reported overpayments. (1) Para 13.(a) refers to the overpayment of $1,098.78 (in total) during the account period 12/1/2018-12/2020 (Account #11). However, using FMV of the asset as of 11/30/2020 (Account #12), not 11/30/2018 (Account #11). (2) Para 13(b) refers to the overpayment of $2,070.90 (to Lathanya) and $1,035.84 (to Merritt Luke). $3,106.74 to both beneficiaries. 3. Need explanation why the overpayment made outside of this accounting period (Dec. 2022 and Jan. 2023) needs to be addressed in this accounting #13. 4. Need allegation regarding the periodic payment to the trustee as to (1) if it is still necessary and (2) if the amount is not to exceed $300 instead of $450. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LORNA S. VILLANUEVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/17/2023. 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 2) Rowena Pascua's address listed on the proof of service is different from her address previously reported to the Court. Please confirm her current address. 3) Please provide a copy of the closing escrow statement for the sale of the estate real property. 4) Has the petitioner taken any IAEA action reportable under CRC 7.250(a)? 5) An allegation must be made as to any assignments or powers of attorney that may affect distributionm, as required by LRSF 14.35(J)(1). 6) The Court has issued an order allowing and settling the deceased executor's final account. Please update a time estimate for closing this estate.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLADYS G. GOSZULAK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/24/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No courtesy copy of the proof of service has been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (4) Joan H. Bello post-deceased the decedent. How are William Bello and Susan Hart successors-in-interest to Joan H. Bello? There was some mention at an earlier hearing about a trust. Who is the trustee of Joan Bello's trust? (5) Why are there other interested parties named in paragraph 11? A will was admitted, and those parties were not beneficiaries and they have not filed claims or requests for special notice (6) What happened to the cash shown in the I&A filed on 5/8/23 if Executor has not taken any actions? A proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues, including the proof of service of the correct, complete notice of the 7/24/23 hearing, must be received by the assigned Examiner by 7/14/23. If they are not timely received by the examiner, an appearance will be required on 7/24/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Item 4 of the I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee WAS APPOINTED.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE S&K WADA TRUST, DATED SEPTEMBER 20, 1995
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Bank Account As Asset Of The Trust
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. (Disclosures not fully made in a petition should simply be addressed in a supplement, not in response to Examiner's Notes, which are not filed or otherwise part of the record or even formally issued. There is also no need to describe in a pleading how the correct form of notice will be mailed; it should just be mailed and a courtesy copy of the proof of service provided to the court. When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAYMOND J. GIORDANI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although (1) no courtesy copy of the papers filed on 4/17/23 or the proof of service filed on 6/13/23 were delivered to the assigned examiner with the other copies on 6/21/23, (2) the declination to serve signed by the decedent's surviving spouse was inexplicably attached to the proof of service, not filed by itself or attached to a supplement to the petition and, therefore, almost overlooked, and (3) only one telephone number was provided on the Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities, when there are two proposed co-administrators. Status of closing the estate is set for 7/8/2024. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before 7/8/24. Appearance will be required on 7/8/24 if no petition has been filed. No SASE was provided; copies of signed orders cannot be returned to Counsel.
